title: Jar Jar Binks's father
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the character who was known as "George R. Binks" in an ambiguously [[canon]] source|the [[George R. Binks|comic]] in which he appears|linebreak=1}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}

{{Quote|Tis only outta respict to yousa mutter un fatter dat my no sentence yous to bean pounded.|Boss Nass|<ref name="EIA" />}}
The '''father''' of the [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] was a Gungan [[Gender/Legends|male]]. Both of Binks' [[Parent/Legends|parents]] had [[Death/Legends|died]] by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. When Binks's clumsiness caused an accident, [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] lessened his punishment out of respect for Binks's [[Binks family|parents]]. The memory of his father saddened him.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Talesstart}}
{{Quote|We Binkses have a tradition that goes back hundreds of years, sailing across the seas on voyages that sometimes take us around the world…|George R. Binks|<ref name="George R. Binks">{{Tales|20|George R. Binks}}</ref>}}
[[File:GRB.jpg|thumb|right|275px|George R. Binks sailing the sea]]
George R. Binks was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] to a family of [[Naboo whale|whalers]]. The [[Binks family]] had practiced the whaler profession for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], so as a mere [[Youngling/Legends|youth]],<ref name="George R. Binks" /> he<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|131|We Need to Talk About Jar Jar}}</ref> commanded his own [[Fisher/Legends|fishing]] gang in his [[City/Legends|hometown]]. At some point, he decided to join the family business, becoming a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Binks & Son Whaling Company]]. In his capacity as a professional whaler, Binks often went on long [[Ocean/Legends|sea]] voyages, even sailing across the entire planet of Naboo on more than one occasion.<ref name="George R. Binks" />

At one point, he fell in love with the warrior-woman [[Sheebla]] and planned on [[Marriage/Legends|marrying]] her, but when he learned of Sheebla's infertility, Binks broke off his wedding plans and left her; to him, a child was more important than love. He eventually married [[Jar Jar Binks's mother|another woman]], not out of love, but because she had the ability to bear him an heir.<ref name="George R. Binks" />
{{Talesend}}

The [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Gender/Legends|male's]] female partner had a son, [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]],<ref name="EIA" /> who was born in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]].<ref name="Insider's Guide">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']] states that [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] is twenty during the events of {{Film|I}}, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Binks therefore must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]].</ref>

{{Talesstart}}
Starting when his son was a child, Binks attempted to teach Jar Jar the whaler profession and involve him in the family company, but much to his chagrin, Jar Jar was uninterested and prone to clumsiness. As a result of these qualities, Binks began resenting his son.<ref name="George R. Binks" />

===Shipwrecked===
{{Quote|These are the bones of my ruined hopes, strewn about this barren rock!!|George R. Binks|<ref name="George R. Binks" />}}
[[File:Naboowhale.jpg|thumb|left|270px|George R. Binks's ship is assailed by a whale.]]
At one point, on a whaling trip with his wife and son in the stormy southernmost seas of Naboo, Binks happened upon a large whale. His initial attempt at [[Harpoon/Legends|harpooning]] the creature was unsuccessful, and in the ensuing fight, wherein he ordered Jar Jar to keep the [[Boat/Legends|ship]] steady, the whale was caught in the rigging of Binks's ship. He issued a series of commands to Jar Jar to turn the wheel starboard and maneuver clear of the situation, but his son was unable to comply. As a result, the ship sank, and the Binks family was forced to seek refuge on a [[Binks's Woe|barren island]].<ref name="George R. Binks" />

A [[Standard month/Legends|month]] later, Binks and his family were still stranded on the island. He grew increasingly annoyed by his son's antics, including picking up a creature that painfully pinched the boy's face. Binks hoped that he would not put the creature in his [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], and when he did, Binks grabbed his son's throat while seizing the creature and insulting Jar Jar. However, Bink's wife came to Jar Jar's defense, reprimanding Binks for taking his anger out on Jar Jar. She added that he never should have given Jar Jar control of the ship's wheel by himself in the first place, but Binks maintained that he had taken the wheel at Jar Jar's age. Bink's despair came to the point that when Jar Jar attempted to show him a supposed "[[sea serpent]]-tailbone" that he had found, Binks slapped the [[Bone/Legends|bone]] away, metaphorically considering the bones to be all of his ruined hopes, scattered across what he considered to be a "barren rock." Immediately after, Binks planted a [[Color/Legends|white]] flag into the sand, naming the island "Binks's Woe."<ref name="George R. Binks" />

Jar Jar then offered to swim for help, claiming to be a good swimmer. Binks initially refused his offer, deeming it a [[Death/Legends|suicidal]] task to swim in the treacherous [[Water/Legends|waters]]. Upon realizing that he could get rid of his son in this manner, Binks encouraged the boy's offer, preparing to toss him into the sea to get him started. However, Binks's attempt at getting Jar Jar killed was cut short when his wife intervened. Just as Binks began sobbing and sat upon the sand, he noticed his son blow into a shell and reached the height of his despair. As a tear dripped from his [[Eye/Legends|eye]], he drew a gun and turned it on himself. His wife tried to stop him but only ended up encouraging him into pulling the trigger by telling him to think of their son. The [[Slug/Legends|bolt]] grazed his skull, knocking him [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]]. As he lay there, he reminisced about his love, Sheebla. As he imagined seeing Sheebla standing before him, his [[Hallucination/Legends|hallucination]] cleared to reveal that it was none other than Jar Jar running on the sand with an [[Octopus/Legends|octopus]] wrapped around his head. Binks's only response was to lower his head in sadness.<ref name="George R. Binks" />
{{Talesend}}

Jar Jar was eventually expelled from all schools in Otoh Gunga and was unable to turn to his parents for guidance; as such, he was forced to take on many odd jobs during his youth.<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Death date" /> both of Binks' parents had died.<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|jarjarbinks|Binks, Jar Jar}}</ref> Also by that [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="32 BBY">''[[Episode I Adventures 9: Rescue in the Core]]'' takes place prior to the events of {{Film|I}}, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> Jar Jar was punished by [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] for an accident that had been caused by Jar Jar's clumsiness; if not for Nass's respect for his parents, the punishment would have been more severe. The mention of his mother and father saddened Jar Jar, but he was thankful that he would not receive a beating.<ref name="EIA" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Talesstart}}
George R. Binks took great pride in his whaling profession, as well as in his family. He was so concerned with securing a male heir to the Binks name that he left the woman he loved because she was infertile. When he finally received a son, Jar Jar, he worked hard to teach the youngster the whaling profession. His son's repeated failures at living up to his expectations became a source of discontent for Binks. His seemingly incompetent son caused him to grow violent and angry, culminating in an unsuccessful attempt at killing himself. He had light brown skin.<ref name="George R. Binks" />

==Skills and abilities==
George R. Binks was an experienced whaler and mariner.<ref name="George R. Binks" />

==Equipment==
Binks wore a green [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]], a white [[shirt]], tan pants, and a black [[Belt/Legends|belt]]. He used a gun and a whaling ship.<ref name="George R. Binks" />
{{Talesend}}

==Behind the scenes==
The father of Jar Jar Binks was first mentioned in the [[2000]] junior novel ''[[Episode I Adventures 9: Rescue in the Core]]'', written by [[Ryder Windham]] as part of the ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Adventures]]'' series.<ref name="EIA" /> The name George R. Binks was created and illustrated by [[Tony Millionaire]] as the main character of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] "[[George R. Binks]]," which was ambiguously [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity and was published in ''[[Star Wars Tales]]''{{'}} [[Star Wars Tales 20|twentieth issue]]<ref name="George R. Binks" /> on [[June 30]], [[2004]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53998/star_wars_tales_1999_20|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #20}}</ref>
